records.ety: [F. matrone, L. matrona, fr. mater mother. See Mother.]

defs: A wife or a widow, especially, one who has borne children; a woman of staid or motherly manners.
quotes: Your wives, your daughters, Your matrons, and your maids.
Grave from her cradle, insomuch that she was a matron before she was a mother.

defs: A housekeeper; esp., a woman who manages the domestic economy of a public institution; a head nurse in a hospital; as, the matron of a school or hospital.
